name: Morelos (Mexico)
description: A white field with the coat of arms of the state of Morelos centred — green ears of corn rising from a brown earth above the motto "Tierra y Libertad".
history: Morelos has no formal flag law of its own; the design shown — a white field bearing the state coat of arms designed by Diego Rivera in 1922 — is in de facto use to represent the state, drawing on the Zapatista banner of the Mexican Revolution.

features.symbolism: A white field charged with the state coat of arms, recalling the banner of Emiliano Zapata's revolutionary movement.

The arms — designed by the muralist Diego Rivera in 1922 — show ears of green corn growing from the earth and the legend "Tierra y Libertad" (Land and Liberty), the watchword of Emiliano Zapata's agrarian revolution, of which Morelos was the heartland.
